law: Law 1: Never Outshine the Master
description: Let leaders feel secure; show your value without making them feel replaced.
moai: Stand tall without blocking the sun.

law: Law 2: Never Put Too Much Trust in Friends, Learn How to Use Enemies
description: Judge people by reliability, not labels. A former rival may have more to prove than a comfortable friend.
moai: Trust the stone that holds, not the one with the familiar name.

law: Law 3: Conceal Your Intentions
description: Share the destination carefully; revealing every move too early invites interference.
moai: The Moai faces forward; it does not announce every footstep.

law: Law 4: Always Say Less Than Necessary
description: Fewer words create fewer mistakes and make each sentence carry more weight.
moai: Heavy stone. Few words.

law: Law 5: So Much Depends on Reputation – Guard It with Your Life
description: Trust takes years to build and minutes to lose, so protect your name with consistent actions.
moai: Your name is carved slowly. Guard the carving.

law: Law 6: Court Attention at All Costs
description: Useful work needs visibility. If nobody notices it, its influence stays small.
moai: Even a great statue needs a visible hill.

law: Law 7: Get Others to Do the Work for You, but Always Take the Credit
description: Use other people's skills and knowledge, but remember that lasting respect comes from giving fair credit.
moai: Many hands move the stone. Honor every hand.

law: Law 8: Make Other People Come to You – Use Bait If Necessary
description: Create value people choose to approach instead of chasing them from a weak position.
moai: Become the landmark; let travelers come to you.

law: Law 9: Win Through Your Actions, Never Through Argument
description: Results persuade better than arguments. Demonstrate the point.
moai: Do not debate the mountain. Climb it.

law: Law 10: Infection: Avoid the Unhappy and Unlucky
description: Moods spread. Keep distance from patterns of constant chaos, not from people who need honest support.
moai: Protect your peace; do not borrow every storm.

law: Law 11: Learn to Keep People Dependent on You
description: Become valuable by building skills and systems that are difficult to replace.
moai: Be useful enough that your absence has weight.

law: Law 12: Use Selective Honesty and Generosity to Disarm Your Victim
description: A timely act of sincerity can lower defenses, but trust should never be used as a trap.
moai: One honest gesture can quiet a noisy room.

law: Law 13: When Asking for Help, Appeal to People's Self-Interest
description: Show people how helping you also helps them.
moai: Show the path where both stones move forward.

law: Law 14: Pose as a Friend, Work as a Spy
description: Listen more than you reveal; information improves judgment.
moai: Big ears, closed mouth, steady eyes.

law: Law 15: Crush Your Enemy Totally
description: Unresolved conflict often returns. Address root causes instead of celebrating a temporary win.
moai: Pull the weed from the root, not just the leaf.

law: Law 16: Use Absence to Increase Respect and Honor
description: Constant availability lowers perceived value. Give people room to notice your absence.
moai: Step away. Let silence explain your value.

law: Law 17: Keep Others in Suspended Terror: Cultivate an Air of Unpredictability
description: If every move is predictable, others can plan around you. Keep some flexibility.
moai: Still does not mean predictable.

law: Law 18: Do Not Build Fortresses to Protect Yourself – Isolation is Dangerous
description: Isolation hides threats and opportunities. Stay connected to people and reality.
moai: A lone stone sees only one horizon.

law: Law 19: Know Who You're Dealing With – Do Not Offend the Wrong Person
description: Different people react differently. Understand temperament before choosing an approach.
moai: Know the ground before planting your weight.

law: Law 20: Do Not Commit to Anyone
description: Protect your independence until a commitment truly serves your values and goals.
moai: Choose your ground before becoming part of it.

law: Law 21: Play a Sucker to Catch a Sucker – Seem Dumber Than Your Mark
description: Understatement can invite others to reveal more. You do not need to prove how clever you are.
moai: Let them underestimate the statue.

law: Law 22: Use the Surrender Tactic: Transform Weakness into Power
description: When direct resistance will destroy you, step back, preserve strength, and change the terrain.
moai: Bend today so you do not break forever.

law: Law 23: Concentrate Your Forces
description: Focused energy beats scattered effort. Put resources where they matter most.
moai: One direction. Full weight.

law: Law 24: Play the Perfect Courtier
description: Influence grows through tact: read the room, respect egos, and make others feel seen.
moai: Move through the room without disturbing every ego.

law: Law 25: Re-Create Yourself
description: Do not let an old identity trap you. Deliberately build the person you need to become.
moai: Carve yourself again when the old shape no longer fits.

law: Law 26: Keep Your Hands Clean
description: Keep your conduct clean and avoid becoming the obvious owner of unnecessary conflict.
moai: Clean hands carry heavier stones.

law: Law 27: Play on People's Need to Believe to Create a Cultlike Following
description: People want meaning and belonging. Never exploit that hunger; earn belief with truth and proof.
moai: Offer meaning, never illusion.

law: Law 28: Enter Action with Boldness
description: Hesitation weakens a good move. Once the decision is sound, act decisively.
moai: Once the path is chosen, move the stone.

law: Law 29: Plan All the Way to the End
description: Think beyond the first win. Consider consequences, reactions, and the final exit.
moai: See the whole road, including where you stop.

law: Law 30: Make Your Accomplishments Seem Effortless
description: Preparation makes difficult work look calm. Let polish show, not panic.
moai: Quiet preparation looks like effortless strength.

law: Law 31: Control the Options: Get Others to Play with the Cards You Deal
description: Shape choices so every available path leads toward an acceptable outcome.
moai: Do not accept bad choices; shape better ones.

law: Law 32: Play to People's Fantasies
description: Facts alone rarely move people. Connect truth to the future they already hope for.
moai: People walk toward a future they can imagine.

law: Law 33: Discover Each Man's Thumbscrew
description: Everyone has motives, fears, and pressure points. Understand them without abusing them.
moai: See the crack; do not strike it without reason.

law: Law 34: Be Royal in Your Own Fashion: Act Like a King to Be Treated Like One
description: Self-respect teaches others how to treat you. Carry yourself with quiet standards.
moai: Carry yourself like something worth respecting.

law: Law 35: Master the Art of Timing
description: The right action at the wrong moment still fails. Watch, wait, then move.
moai: The Moai waits longer than the wave.

law: Law 36: Disdain Things You Cannot Have: Ignoring Them is the Best Revenge
description: Chasing what rejects you gives it power. Redirect attention toward what remains possible.
moai: What you cannot hold should not hold your attention.

law: Law 37: Create Compelling Spectacles
description: People remember vivid experiences. Present important ideas in a form they can feel and recall.
moai: Make the lesson visible enough to remember.

law: Law 38: Think as You Like but Behave Like Others
description: Keep your inner independence while understanding the social language around you.
moai: Keep your mind; learn the village rhythm.

law: Law 39: Stir Up Waters to Catch Fish
description: Strong emotion clouds judgment. Stay calm while others reveal themselves.
moai: Be the still water while others splash.

law: Law 40: Despise the Free Lunch
description: Free offers often carry hidden obligations. Know the real price before accepting.
moai: Every free rope may be tied to something.

law: Law 41: Avoid Stepping into a Great Man's Shoes
description: Do not imitate a legend. Build a distinct identity that can stand beside the legacy.
moai: Honor the old statue. Carve your own face.

law: Law 42: Strike the Shepherd and the Sheep Will Scatter
description: Groups often organize around a central source of conflict. Address the source, not every symptom.
moai: Find the drumbeat guiding the crowd.

law: Law 43: Work on the Hearts and Minds of Others
description: People support what they feel part of. Win trust before demanding action.
moai: Move hearts first; feet follow.

law: Law 44: Disarm and Infuriate with the Mirror Effect
description: Reflecting someone's behavior back to them can reveal patterns words cannot.
moai: Become the mirror, not the argument.

law: Law 45: Preach the Need for Change, but Never Reform Too Much at Once
description: Change works when people can absorb it. Move firmly, but not so fast that everyone breaks.
moai: Move the island one stone at a time.

law: Law 46: Never Appear Too Perfect
description: Visible perfection breeds distance and envy. Let people see enough humanity to trust you.
moai: A small crack makes the statue believable.

law: Law 47: Do Not Go Past the Mark You Aimed For; In Victory, Learn When to Stop
description: Victory can create overconfidence. Define the finish line before you begin.
moai: Know when the stone has reached the hill.

law: Law 48: Assume Formlessness
description: Rigid plans break under change. Stay adaptable while keeping your purpose.
moai: Purpose stays. Shape changes.
